GUJARAT PIPAVAV PORT: Q1 FY27 OPERATIONAL UPDATE 🚢📦

• 📦 Container volume increased to 168k TEUs in Q1 FY27 from 164k TEUs YoY, reflecting steady container handling growth 

• 🚗 Ro-Ro cargo handled surged 55% YoY to 65,000 units from 42,000 units, indicating strong automobile logistics demand 

• ⚓ Dry bulk cargo stood at 0.52 million MT, broadly stable compared to 0.55 million MT in the year-ago quarter 

• 🛢️ Liquid cargo volumes declined to 0.22 million MT from 0.41 million MT YoY, while container train volumes also moderated to 346 trains 

• 🚆 Rail-linked container volumes were 88k TEUs, compared with 99k TEUs in Q1 FY26, reflecting softer rail evacuation during the quarter 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Strong growth in container and Ro-Ro volumes offsets weakness in liquid cargo and rail-linked container movement, indicating a mixed operational performance.)

reports.text:
CRIZAC: PARTNERS WITH ETS INDIA TO EXPAND TOEFL & GRE PREPARATION ACCESS 🎓🌍

• 🤝 Crizac has partnered with ETS India to provide authorised TOEFL® and GRE® preparation resources to more than 1 lakh study-abroad aspirants across India 

• 🌐 The partnership combines ETS' globally recognised assessments with Crizac's network of 14,000+ recruitment partners, 500+ universities across 14+ countries, and support for ~4 lakh student applications annually 

• 🎯 Students will receive access to official preparation resources along with personalised counselling, improving readiness for admissions to global universities 

• 📍 ETS plans to expand its official TOEFL & GRE test centres in India from 65+ to 100 over the next year, with a third authorised test centre in Kolkata planned with Crizac in 2027 

• 📚 The collaboration is aimed at improving access to trusted study-abroad guidance, especially for students from Tier-II and Tier-III cities 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Crizac's study-abroad ecosystem, enhances service offerings and could support higher student engagement and partner growth.)

MAGELLANIC CLOUD: WINS ₹6.93 CR RAILWAY SURVEILLANCE ORDER 🚆📹

• 🏗️ Wholly-owned subsidiary Provigil Surveillance Ltd. received a Letter of Acceptance (LOA) worth ₹6.93 crore from South Central Railway, Vijayawada Division

• 🎥 Order involves design, supply, installation, testing & commissioning of AI-enabled IP-based CCTV surveillance systems across multiple railway stations

• 🚉 Project covers key stations including Vijayawada, Rajahmundry, Nellore, Kakinada and Gudur, along with signalling & telecom infrastructure

• 🤖 Strengthens the company's focus on AI-powered surveillance, Video Management Systems (VMS) and intelligent public safety solutions

• ⏳ Project execution period is 12 months and follows another recent surveillance order from the same railway division

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens railway order book, reinforces execution capabilities and expands presence in AI-enabled surveillance solutions.)

TATA CAPITAL: TO ACQUIRE 88.6% STAKE IN YOGAKSHEMAM LOANS 🏦🤝

• 📑 Board approved acquisition of ~88.6% stake in Yogakshemam Loans Ltd through a Securities Subscription & Purchase Agreement (SSPA)

• 💰 Deal includes purchase of shares from existing shareholders and subscription to fresh equity, subject to regulatory approvals

• 🏦 Target is a Base Layer NBFC focused on gold loans, with ~₹708 crore AUM and branch network across Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh

• 📈 FY26 target financials: Revenue ₹140.39 crore and PAT ₹14.21 crore

• ⏳ Transaction is expected to be completed within 8 months, including RBI approval

🟢 Impact: Positive (Acquisition strengthens Tata Capital's retail lending franchise and expands its presence in the gold loan segment.)

**Key Takeaway**

**NMDC | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Lump ore price cut by ₹250/t.
- Fines price cut by ₹150/t.
- Including taxes: ₹310/t & ₹190/t cut.
- Lump price: ₹6,745/t.
- Fines price: ₹5,815/t.
- Domestic fines at 50% discount.
- Five-year average discount: 56%.

**Industry View**
- Seaborne iron ore prices softened.
- Supply increased globally.
- Chinese demand remained weak.
- Iron ore at ~US$98/t.
- Prices down 7% since May-end.

**Key Takeaway**
- **NMDC's price cuts reflect weaker global iron ore prices, driven by higher seaborne supply and softer Chinese demand, while domestic prices continue to trade at a significant discount to import parity.**

reports.text:
**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | MOSL View**

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,800.
- Revenue grew 15% YoY.
- LFL growth at 5.5%.

**Key Highlights**
- Metro stores remained flat.
- Non-metro stores grew well.
- Gross margin expanded 45 bps.
- Higher GM&A mix supported margins.
- Employee cost rose 32% YoY.
- Added 3 stores in Q1FY27.
- Store expansion remains key.
- DMart Ready exited 7 cities.
- Presence reduced to 11 cities.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL remains positive on Avenue Supermarts, supported by healthy profitability, continued store expansion and stronger non-metro performance despite slower LFL growth.**

Indian EV component market is set to grow 8x to 3.55L cr market in 2032

Biggest beneficiary is Sona BLW with 75% order book of 26200cr order book is from EV from Tesla ,byd & almost all European Auto major EV program

In SME OBSC perfection leads the way with 350cr+ EV order
Smartworks leases 930+ seats in Pune, with the 60-month deal expected to generate ~₹58 crore in rental revenue

**AVENUE SUPERMARTS (DMART) | Jefferies View**

**Brokerage View**
- Hold rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,500.
- Q1 better than headline suggests.
- Q4 pre-buying impacted Q1.

**Key Highlights**
- Older metro stores flat YoY.
- Quick commerce remains a concern.
- Gross margin at multi-quarter high.
- Staff cost rose over 30%.
- EBITDA margin expansion limited.
- DMart Ready exited 7 cities.
- Now present in 11 cities.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains neutral on Avenue Supermarts, noting resilient underlying demand but highlighting quick commerce pressure, rising employee costs and a narrower DMart Ready footprint.**

Emkay note:

Emkay Morning Insights (13/07)

Macro: Markets largely looking through the renewed US-Iran escalation. 

However, any disruption to the Strait of Hormuz remains a key upside risk for crude and global supply chains. 

Internet: Pranav's deep-dive on Zepto highlights that while it has become India's second-largest quick commerce player with industry-leading orders per store, profitability remains the missing piece. 

he continues to prefer Eternal (Blinkit) and Swiggy given their stronger unit economics and clearer path to sustainable earnings.

Banks: Q1FY27 should see another quarter of healthy credit growth despite near-term NIM pressure from elevated funding costs. 

Asset quality remains resilient, with Axis Bank, HDFC Bank, IndusInd Bank, IDFC First Bank, Canara Bank and Indian Bank expected to deliver relatively stronger performance.

Results:

DMART reported an in-line but uninspiring quarter as slowing same-store growth, modest store additions and continued DMart Ready rationalisation reinforced concerns around growth and valuation. 

LTM delivered a mixed Q1 with muted revenue growth but better margins, healthy deal in wins and improving AI traction, with management guiding for stronger growth through the rest of FY27

ANTONY WASTE: 9 EMPLOYEES LOSE LIVES IN LANDFILL TRAGEDY; WTE PLANT TEMPORARILY SHUT 🙏⚠️

• 🌧️ Nine employees lost their lives after heavy rains triggered a landfill collapse, damaging the administrative building at the company's Waste-to-Energy plant 

• 🚑 14 employees were rescued and are receiving medical treatment and counselling 

• 💰 Company will provide ₹25 lakh financial assistance to each bereaved family, along with medical, funeral and insurance support, employment to an eligible family member, and education support for minor children 

• ⚙️ Waste-to-Energy plant operations have been temporarily suspended pending structural and safety assessment; municipal waste collection and transportation services continue uninterrupted 

• 🛡️ Company stated that its assets and operations are adequately insured, while the financial impact is being assessed and will be disclosed later 

🔴 Impact: Negative (Temporary suspension of the WTE plant and uncertainty over the financial impact may weigh on sentiment, though core waste collection operations remain unaffected.)

reports.text:
SMARTWORKS: UK-BASED GLOBAL SERVICES FIRM EXPANDS PUNE PRESENCE 🏢📈

• 🇬🇧 Indian subsidiary of a UK-headquartered global professional services & technology firm has leased 930+ seats in Pune with Smartworks 

• 🪑 Client's total seat count with Smartworks has now crossed 1,730 seats 

• 💰 New 60-month lease is expected to generate ~₹58 crore of committed rental revenue; total commitment with the client rises to ~₹102 crore 

• 🏢 Enterprise clients with 300+ seats contribute ~69% of rental revenue, while 90%+ of revenue comes from enterprise customers 

• 🌍 As of 31 March 2026, Smartworks operated 66 centres across 15 cities in India & Singapore with ~16.1 million sq. ft. under management 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large enterprise expansion improves revenue visibility and reinforces Smartworks' leadership in managed office spaces.)

reports.text:
CLSA on IRB

O-P, TP Rs 34.5

Key message from IRB Infra’s 1QFY27 tolling trends was overall toll growth had improved 12% (7% in 1QFY26) as June 2026 tolls rose 15% YoY.

Notably, toll growth more than doubled in 1QFY27 at its 100%-owned Mumbai-Pune e-way (9% YoY versus 4% in 1QFY26), while tolls at its Infrastructure Trust (InvIT) assets rose 11%

IRB, India’s No.1 toll platform, has steam left from re-leveraging its equity

**LTIMINDTREE | DAM Capital View**

**Brokerage View**
- Neutral rating.
- Target Price: ₹4,050.
- Q1 CC revenue up 0.3% QoQ.
- CC revenue up 6.4% YoY.
- EBIT margin at 15.5%.

**Key Highlights**
- Banking grew 3.2% QoQ.
- Technology grew 3.4% QoQ.
- Production declined 5.7% QoQ.
- North America led growth.
- Europe & APAC declined.
- Margin up 40 bps QoQ.
- Utilization improved 70 bps.
- Deal wins: USD 1.68 billion.
- Book-to-bill ratio: 1.4x.

**Key Takeaway**
- **DAM Capital remains Neutral on LTIMindtree, supported by healthy margins and deal wins, while mixed vertical performance and regional weakness keep the outlook balanced.**

Jefferies on Avenue Supermart

Hold, TP Rs 4500

1Q appears better than the headline suggests, given 4Q saw prebuying that pulled demand forward, making 1Q LFL seem somewhat softer than it actually was

However, mgmt’s comment that older & high throughput metro stores were flat YoY is a bit of a concern (could some of it be due to Q/C?)

GM rose to a multi-qtr high, but >30% growth in staff cost limited YoY EBITDA margin expansion.

DMart Ready exited 7 more cities & is now in 11 (peak at 25)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
JPM on Avenue Supermart

Neutral, TP cut to Rs 4250

Q1 EBITDA margin was stable (+10 bp y/y) backed by gross margin expansion (+50bp y/y; salience of General Merchandise & Apparel improved).

However, LFL growth to 5.5% (despite rising inflation) was a key negative, with older stores in large metro cities posting weak flat growth indicating the higher-than-anticipated adverse impact of quick commerce.

Non-metro stores continue to fare better, further supporting DMART’s focus to incrementally add more stores in these cities - Q1 saw 3 new store adds, with 2 being in non-metros, taking overall count to 503 stores.

Given weak LFL print & stable margin delivery, expect stock performance to stay soft.

**LTIMINDTREE | Citi View**

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating.
- Target Price: ₹3,450.
- Q1 revenue flattish QoQ.
- PAT supported by one-time gain.
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR: 5%.
- Trades at 19.5x FY27 earnings.

**Key Highlights**
- EBIT supported by lower SG&A.
- Subcontracting costs increased.
- TTM TCV up 7% YoY.
- Headcount growth moderated.
- Management confident on Q2.
- Momentum expected into H2.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains cautious on LTIMindtree despite positive management commentary, citing modest organic growth expectations and reliance on one-time factors in Q1 performance.**

EARNINGS & BUSINESS UPDATES

Results
- LTIMindtree (Q1FY27, QoQ)
  - Revenue: ₹11,608 Cr (+2.8%).
  - EBIT: ₹1,799 Cr (+5.3%).
  - EBIT margin: 15.5%.

- Avenue Supermarts (Q1FY27, YoY)

  - Lalit Ahuja appointed COO.

- Just Dial (Q1FY27, YoY)

  - EBITDA: ₹87.4 Cr (+1.2%).
  - EBITDA margin: 26.7%.
  - PAT: ₹166 Cr (+4.1%).

- Avantel (Q1FY27, YoY)
  - Revenue: ₹70.4 Cr (+35.7%).
  - EBITDA: ₹17.4 Cr (+66.7%).
  - EBITDA margin: 24.7%.
  - PAT: ₹5.4 Cr (+67.4%).

Business Updates
- Fino Payments Bank
  - New accounts up 31% YoY.
  - Average deposits: ₹2,755 Cr (+11%).
  - Active users up 38% YoY.
  - Loan referrals up 253% YoY.

- Adani Green
  - Operational capacity: 20,142 MW (+27%).
  - Added 848 MW in Q1.
  - Energy sales up 30% YoY.
  - BESS capacity: 3,551 MWh.
  - FY27E BESS target: 10+ GWh.
  - 50 GW renewable target by 2030.

- L&T Finance
  - Highest-ever PAT: ₹902 Cr.
  - Loan book: ₹1,29,634 Cr (+27%).
  - NIMs + Fees: 10.47%.
  - Credit cost improved to 2.54%.

reports.text:
​Historically, Indian mining was defined by heavy, asset-heavy "merchant miners" digging up bulky ferrous commodities (iron ore and manganese) tied directly to domestic infrastructure and the steel cycle.

 Today, the industry has evolved into a landscape driven by deep-value forward integration, de-risked capital reallocation, and globalized critical mineral and precious metal hunting.

​The shift is visible across three major evolutionary steps:

​Company 1: The "Volume & Value Integration" Play — Lloyds Metals

​Five years ago, Lloyds Metals was primarily perceived as a struggling regional mining and DRI (Direct Reduced Iron) player. The massive shift in its thesis came via the MMDR Amendment Act, the appointment of Thriveni Earthmovers as MDO (Mine Developer and Operator), and an aggressive push to ramp up the Surjagarh iron ore mine.

​What Changed: Lloyds proved that merchant mining in India is no longer just about extracting and selling raw ore fines. By scaling up mining limits (targeting 26 MTPA to 55 MTPA ROM) and simultaneously laying down massive slurry pipelines and building pellet and wire rod plants, they shifted the playbook. 

​The Industry Signal: Success in modern Indian mining requires bypassing broken logistics via captive infrastructure (like slurry pipelines) and insulating margins from raw commodity cycles by transforming from a pure miner into an integrated steel and value-added materials ecosystem.  

​Company 2: The Cash Cow Play — Sandur Manganese

​Sandur Manganese & Iron Ores represents the classic, conservative Indian mining house with high-quality, low-cost captive reserves in the semi-arid Dharwar Craton. Enjoying the commodity upcycles of the early 2020s, Sandur built a fortress balance sheet with massive cash reserves.  

​What Changed: Rather than endlessly plowing cash back into highly regulated, cyclical, and carbon-heavy mining expansions, Sandur executed a stark pivot. Following a major strategic review, the board initiated a rebranding exercise to diversify capital allocation away from concentrated mining risk into asset-light or structural growth sectors like Hospitality, Education, and Medical Devices. 

​The Industry Signal: Legacy miners recognize that regulatory friction, environmental compliance, and commodity price volatility make pure-play mining a lumpy business. The new playbook uses mining as an annuity cash engine to fund cleaner, higher-multiple, non-cyclical corporate avatars.

​Company 3: The "Asset-Light Global Frontier" Play - Deccan Gold Mines
​Deccan Gold Mines represents the newest frontier of Indian mining—transitioning from a decades-long "exploration-only" micro-cap into a diversified, production-oriented global platform for precious and critical minerals.  

​What Changed: DGML broke the structural gridlock of Indian gold exploration by going asset-light, utilizing rights issues, and building a globally diversified portfolio. They moved into execution at the Jonnagiri Gold Project in Andhra Pradesh and the Altyn Tor project in Kyrgyzstan (targeting commercial production), while aggressively acquiring exposure to the green energy transition—such as their acquisition of a controlling stake in a Spanish Tungsten project and discoveries of Nickel-Copper-PGE in Chhattisgarh.  

​The Industry Signal: The modern mining company isn't bound by local geographies or traditional bulk commodities. Driven by the global electric vehicle transition and semiconductor demand, the focus has shifted toward high-margin, security-sensitive elements (Lithium, Tungsten, Tantalum, Cobalt, and Gold).

INDIA GROWTH RIDING ON COPPER GOVT SHOULD HELP BOOST CAPACITY
Sunita Tools revised projections.

Businesses I was reading about this week, things that I heard and found interesting across consumption, textiles , Pharmaceuticals and Wires  (11/7/26) Market sense note :

Indo Count Industries:

 FY26 remained centred around 3 key priorities: First, protecting market share. Volume and core business revenues remained subdued and volatile during the year due to the evolving implications of the U.S. tariff situation

Utility bedding, US branded business is what they are focusing on, they are seeing better wallet share gains and also incremental access to customers, their new greenfield North Carolina Facility has also been commenced ( 13 million to 31 million pillows per annum capacity growth), 3 facilities in US , expecting 65% Total utilisation from US in F27, branded business Wamsuta (heritage brand ) , total licensed brands now : 6
Again, similar theme: Completion of heavy investment phase now moving into sweating the assets phase

They are expecting F27 to be a record year with INR5,500 crores, implying revenue growth of over 30% in FY27 compared to FY26, EBITDA margins at 13% , this is the guidance post considering RM prices , plus US with increase in prices they haven’t seen demand destruction

With all of these things coming in place , targeting 8,000 cr revenue by calendar year 2028, which could mean substantial growth in the context of textiles and alot of tailwinds may also come from the FTA'S when in action , textiles also has lot of baggage of no returns 

Bansal Wiring: Largest Stainless Steel Wire manufacturers in India by volume 3,000+ SKUs – highest among all steel wire manufacturers in India 5,000+ Customers across various industries and FY26 capacity utilization: management stated ~67–68%.

This year expecting 400 crores of Cash Flow, ROCE improvement to 25% targets in the next few years ( currently 15% )

1st quarter F27, specialty wire we are getting trial orders at better margins, post 4 stage validations we have 2 lakh tonnes target, this will be trajectory changing product for the company , no customer concentration , no industry/customer is major risk to the company , we are also seeing the demand trend stabilising , expecting Q1 to be weaker on margins ( cost + Lag 35 days )  , and 3 quarters ahead will be 20% growth , focusing on cash generation aggressively for incremental growth , this cash to growth capex loop is what they are trying to master

Constantly focusing on market share gains, we have lot of experience in the ecosystem that gives us a lot of confidence to grow

Specialty wires currently <3–4% with expectation to start forming part of mix going forward , which is a classic value added product thesis for better margins that involve incremental volumes on realisations being better hence changing the mix positively , weak Q1 is consensus expectation is here pretty much , growth is second half of the year

3) Gufic Biosciences , maybe ready for the next leg of operating leverage 

Few things I like about Gufic Bioscience’s Q4 numbers and why there may be an inflection point if they can execute Indore   :
1) Management is very focused and can correct errors very quick
2) Focusing on the right quality of growth and focusing on R&D
3) Building an innovator DNA that will help them for scalability
Some interesting insights from the recent concall :
1) botulinum toxin front, we are now firmly the number 2 brand in India, sitting at approximately 23% market share in a market where the innovator holds dominant position.
2) Q4 is that we formally signed an in-licensing agreement with a leading Canadian aesthetics company, one that holds a significant market share in U.S. for fillers and biostimulators. This deal fills what was the biggest gap in our aesthetic portfolio, a credible filler offering. Doctors who currently hesitate to shift their full aesthetic business to us because we offer only a toxin will now have a reason to consolidate with Gufic (this will help them fill lot of gaps on the exports side , as the spaces keep getting filled)
3) Expecting some more improvement in the gross margins ( 0.5% - 1%)
4) Navsari - Cartridge and Pen GLP-1 production, Indore, we have signed an agreement with another player who will be focusing on the lyophilized vial form (Hetero Drugs )
5) There is scope for market expansion as well Botulinum Toxin with the fillers and toxins usually are with the same source and hence with more tie ups and collaborations the ratio of Indian population still leaves good headroom for incremental total market expansion here as well
5) 15 % Market guidance for revenue growth , but I think they can do much more with Indore reaching breakeven and now incremental exports as well , 15% I would call conservative by the management, and on the margins front EBITDA margin for '26, '27 should be in the range of around 18%. And over a year -- year-by-year, there will be improvement of about between 0.5% to 1% over the year-to-year to come. And it can go up to by 2030, it can go above 20%
( disc: biased in Gufic , no reco, bucket of salt opinion )

Some small and interesting things I also read this week :
1) Bluejet QIP has some very interesting names – Shamyak and ICICI prudential have lapped up all the shares , now Shamyak for some of you who may not know is an investment vehicle of the ENAM group , they had initially purchased the stock in IPO , exited it at 800 ( this was in another vehicle BDPROP ) , and now entering again , we have owned it from IPO too and have seen this whole journey , do read my Q4 call notes to see the latest on Bluejet
2) Laser Power and Infra and SBI funds management: After a while these 2 IPO’S seem to be pretty reasonably priced and seem to have left some value on the table , have written a detailed notes on Laser Power and SBI fund management , I don’t think a note is necessary , by now all of you are very well versed with the Fund management business ( Again both companies have very strong anchor books )
3) Ideaforge QIP has also closed , you all know this was our peak pessimism play last year and it has doubled from where I had written on it , but now this QIP has some very interesting names Ashish Kacholia , some FPI’S as well and Bandhan MF has picked up some shares here apart from the usual names , again in this space it is all about execution , capability depth is tough to map in this sector

4) Credit Growth numbers are very strong and cannot be ignored we have already started to see that pricing in many NBFC'S and MFI'S as well with some very interesting updates on Insurance also , more than that I think about it from an FII ownership POV , banks and insurance are the easiest for them to buy because they can absorb this amount of money , if that starts to comeback in growth and IT stops bleeding , we could be looking at very good numbers for H2FY27 NIFTY 50 EPS growth as well

This is a newer experiment of writing my market sense weekly note folks if you liked it do let me know , can continue this format , thats all for this one hope you liked reading this one

This small-cap CDMO is quietly building for its next phase of growth.

Influx Healthtech has shared a strong Q1 FY27 business update, and a few things stood out.

 - Management has guided for 25–30% revenue growth in FY27.
 - EBITDA margins are expected to remain healthy at 20–22%.
 - Its nutraceutical manufacturing facility is being expanded from 35,000 sq. ft. to 75,000 sq. ft., with commissioning expected from August 2026.
 - Once fully operational, the company's installed manufacturing capacity is expected to increase by 2.5x.

But the story goes beyond just capacity expansion.

The company is also investing across multiple high-growth verticals:
 - New tablet granulation line for nutraceuticals.
 - High-capacity RTD beverage manufacturing infrastructure.
 - Major expansion in pet nutrition and pet healthcare products.
 - New cosmetic manufacturing equipment to launch differentiated formulations.
 - Export expansion after regulatory approvals in Tanzania, with further focus on Africa.

Another interesting point is the company's diversification.
It now manufactures 3,400+ SKUs across nutraceuticals, beverages, functional nutrition, cosmetics and pet care, reducing dependence on any single product category.

If management delivers on its guidance, FY27 could be a strong operating year backed by both capacity expansion and improving product mix.

The key monitorable now will be how quickly the new facility ramps up and whether the company can sustain its guided margins while growing at 25–30%.

Influx Healthtech is gradually evolving from a niche CDMO into a broader health and wellness manufacturing platform.

GOVT RULES OUT PURE PETROL, E10; E20 TO REMAIN INDIA’S STANDARD FUEL

The Centre has said it will not offer consumers a choice between pure petrol, E10 and E20. According to the Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas, maintaining three parallel petrol grades across India’s fuel distribution network would be operationally complex, costly, and would undermine the significant investments already made in ethanol production and blending infrastructure.

reports.text:
We are pleasantly surprised by Q1FY27 business updates so far:

Credit growth - accelerating and healthy

Consumer (Retailers, auto OEMs, jewelers, FMCG, real estate) - YoY growth robust despite the overhang of war in Q1 

USD$ services exports - YoY growth remains healthy despite deflationary impact of GenAI. Leasing data, office occupancy, square foot addition suggest that India is moving from back office to middle office. IT services revenue growth remains lackluster but GCCs (so far) are more than picking up the slack

Sunrise industries galore - Precision manufacturing, drones, specialty chemicals, CDMOs etc

Capital goods - Domestic business accelerating as India builds domestic DC footprint

Markets haven't moved for 22 months now in local currency terms. Valuations have time corrected. Growth trajectory remains healthy and more importantly, untethered to the AI capex build-out.

Hard to argue that India isn't interesting again for FIIs

Interesting to WATCH Q1 Number of FINANCE And Banking side
Transmission sector Capacity addition mapping and delta

📊 PondyOxides --- The next leg of growth may be bigger than the last one

For years, Pondy Oxides was largely seen as a lead recycling company.

FY26 changed that picture.

Revenue grew 45%, while EBITDA and PAT more than doubled. Over the last five years, PAT has compounded at 67% annually.

But the more interesting part is what management is building next.

1. Lead capacity is ready for the next jump

Lead recycling capacity has expanded from 1.32 lakh tonnes to 2.04 lakh tonnes.

Against FY26 sales of around 1.01 lakh tonnes, management is targeting 1.25–1.30 lakh tonnes in FY27, supported by new customers and products.

This means much of the required capacity is already in place. The next step is utilisation.

2. Copper is becoming the second growth engine

Copper recycling capacity was doubled from 6,000 to 12,000 tonnes during FY26.

Management expects 8,000–9,000 tonnes from the existing recycling business in FY27, giving copper a much larger contribution to the overall business.

3. Copper cathode is the real re-rating trigger

POCL is setting up a 36,000-tonne copper cathode facility in two phases.

The first 18,000-tonne phase is expected by December 2026, with the project funded through internal accruals.

Management expects copper cathode EBITDA per tonne of around ₹60,000–70,000, compared with ₹35,000–40,000 from the existing copper business.

FY27 may see only an initial contribution. FY28 should reflect the more meaningful full-year benefit.

4. Moving from volumes to value addition

Value-added products already contribute around 65% of lead revenue.

POCL manufactures niche customer-specific alloys, allowing it to earn better premiums than regular recycled lead.

This was visible in Q4, when lead volumes moderated but EBITDA per tonne increased 43%.

5. Plastics turnaround and Mundra optionality

The relocated plastics unit has turned PAT positive, and management expects it to remain profitable in FY27.

The company also plans to begin work on its Mundra project during FY27.

Mundra and newer recycling verticals provide optionality, although management has not yet provided detailed financial guidance.

6. Growth without large balance-sheet stress

FY27 capex is expected at ₹180–200 Cr, largely towards copper and downstream integration.

Management does not plan to take long-term debt for the announced expansion and is targeting working-capital days below 45.

That becomes important as the business scales.

7. Target 2030 remains ambitious

Management is targeting:

• 15%+ sustained volume growth  
• 20%+ revenue CAGR  
• EBITDA margin above 8%  
• ROCE above 20%  
• Value-added contribution above 60%

The company is gradually transforming from a lead recycler into a diversified, value-added non-ferrous recycling platform.

Short-term trigger:

The record date for the stock split is July 21.

Every 2 shares of ₹5 face value will become 5 shares of ₹2 each.

The lower post-split price can improve liquidity and retail participation, which may keep the stock’s all-time high in focus in the near term.

But the split itself does not create value.

The bigger triggers remain the lead ramp-up, copper cathode commissioning and margin improvement through value-added products.

Key watchpoints: project execution, imported scrap availability and working-capital control.

Not a recommendation.

TAHMAR ENTERPRISES: LAUNCHES 'TAHMAR ENERGY PLATFORM', ENTERS RENEWABLE ENERGY BUSINESS ☀️⚡

• 🚀 Launched the "Tahmar Energy Platform", marking the company's entry into the renewable energy sector as a new business division.

• 🌞 The platform enables consumers to join co-operative solar societies and own identified offsite solar generation capacity, with electricity benefits adjusted against their consumption.

• 🤝 Appointed Axel Renewables Corp LLP and other service providers on an arm's-length basis for market development, customer acquisition, technical, regulatory and operational support.

• 📍 The initial project will be developed in Maharashtra, with the first solar farm targeted for completion by Q4 CY2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

• 🌱 The initiative aims to monetise the company's existing land, expand into green energy and build a scalable renewable energy platform. The financial impact will be disclosed once it crystallises.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marks diversification into the high-growth renewable energy sector with a scalable solar platform and long-term growth potential.)

FINO PAYMENTS BANK: JUNE BUSINESS UPDATE SHOWS STRONG DEPOSIT & LOAN GROWTH 🏦📈

• 👥 Opened 3.13 lakh new deposit accounts in June, up 31% YoY, taking the total account base to 1.8 crore.

• 💰 Average total deposits grew 11% YoY to ₹2,755 crore, while renewal income increased 6% YoY to ₹20.8 crore.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong growth in deposits, digital engagement and loan referrals offsets lower transaction throughput, indicating healthy underlying business momentum.)

FINO PAYMENTS BANK: HIGHLIGHTS SFB PREPARATION & STRONG LOAN REFERRAL GROWTH 🏦📈

• 🏦 Loan referral disbursals surged 3.5x YoY to ~₹240 crore, strengthening the bank's proposed Small Finance Bank (SFB) transition strategy.

• 💸 Transaction business (Remittance, Micro ATM & AePS) continued to see throughput moderation due to the industry-wide shift from cash to UPI.

reports.text:
Rathi Steel & Power Delivers Strong Q1 FY27 Update; Sales Volume Rises ~30% YoY, Revenue Surpasses ₹193 Cr

Rathi Steel & Power Limited delivered a strong operational Update in Q1 FY27, supported by robust sales volume growth, higher turnover, and a diversified product portfolio across Stainless Steel products and MS TMT Rebars, despite challenging market conditions.

Q1 FY27 Highlights :
* Sales Volume: 28,372 MT (~30% YoY)
* Revenue: ₹193+ Cr (24%+ YoY)
- TMT Rebar Sales: 18,677 MT (More than 2x YoY)

The TMT Rebar segment emerged as a key growth driver, with sales volumes increasing from 8,295 MT in Q1 FY26 to 18,677 MT in Q1 FY27. The strong scale-up strengthened the Company’s overall product mix and provided greater flexibility to respond to varying demand conditions across segments.

The Company delivered this growth despite geopolitical uncertainties, volatile energy costs, and fluctuating demand momentum. Going forward, it remains focused on optimising its product mix, expanding market reach, capitalising on emerging opportunities, and maintaining disciplined cost management amid an evolving global operating environment.

• 🏆 Declared the successful bidder under Tariff Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) for the "Transmission System for Integration of Krishnagiri REZ Phase-I" project. 

• 📩 Received the Letter of Intent (LoI) on July 10, 2026. 

• 🏗️ Project will be executed on a Build, Own, Operate and Transfer (BOOT) basis. 

• ⚡ Scope includes setting up 2 new 765/400 kV substations in Andhra Pradesh. 

• 🌐 Also includes construction of 765 kV and 400 kV transmission lines across Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Karnataka, along with associated bays. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens POWERGRID's transmission project pipeline and supports renewable energy integration.)

RUSTOMJEE: Q1 FY27 BUSINESS UPDATE 🏗️

• 🏠 Pre-sales at ₹617 crore, down 42% YoY, as there were no new launches during Q1; sales were supported by sustenance demand. 

• 💰 Collections rose 4% YoY to ₹599 crore, reflecting healthy cash flows from previously sold units.

ESHA MEDIA RESEARCH: ANNOUNCES MANAGEMENT TRANSITION, TO BUILD AI-LED MEDIA INTELLIGENCE PLATFORM 🤖📡

• 👨‍💼 Completed management transition, with Siddharth Saraf appointed as Managing Director and Rakesh Mudgal as Executive Director & CFO; founding team moves to advisory roles. 

• 🤖 Repositioning from a traditional media monitoring company to an AI-native, end-to-end media & reputation intelligence platform. 

• 📺 Platform will provide monitoring, analytics and intelligence across TV, print, digital news, social media, influencers and podcasts. 

• 🏢 Solutions will cater to corporates, listed companies, brands, sports teams, public institutions and government agencies, covering sentiment analysis, crisis alerts, competitive intelligence and reputation management. 

• 🚀 Company has started platform modernisation, AI product development, talent hiring and strategic partnerships, with further product roadmap updates expected in the coming quarters. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic transformation into an AI-led media intelligence platform expands addressable market and creates new long-term growth opportunities.)

PNGS GARGI FASHION JEWELLERY: Q1 FY27 BUSINESS UPDATE 💎📈

• 💰 Q1 Revenue from Operations rose 11.6% YoY to ₹30.48 crore, driven by continued consumer demand and retail network expansion. 

• 🪔 Akshaya Tritiya sales surged 77.4% YoY to around ₹2.37 crore, compared with ₹1.33 crore last year.

• 💰 Priced US$300 million Floating Rate Notes (FRNs) due 2029 under its US$8 billion Global Medium Term Note Programme 

• 📅 Tenor of 3 years, maturing on 16 July 2029

Cupid Limited: Expanding into a Broader Consumer Wellness Business

Cupid Limited has evolved beyond being a contraceptive manufacturer into a diversified consumer wellness and global institutional products company. With a growing branded FMCG portfolio in India and an established export business serving over 125 countries, the Company is building multiple growth drivers supported by manufacturing expansion, product innovation, and wider market reach.

A Distinctive Two-Engine Business Model
* Established global institutional business with exports to 125+ countries
* Rapidly expanding branded FMCG and consumer wellness business in India
* One of the few Indian companies with a meaningful presence across both global institutional markets and the domestic branded consumer segment

Diversified revenue streams supporting long-term business growth
* Profitable Exports Supporting Consumer Growth
* Branded FMCG portfolio generated ₹120+ crore revenue in FY26
* Products available across approximately 1.7 lakh retail outlets
* Consumer business expansion supported by cash flows from the Company's established global export operations
* Balanced growth model supported by a profitable export business while expanding its branded consumer portfolio

Multiple Growth Drivers Coming Together
* Palava manufacturing capacity expansion expected to become operational during FY27

* Expanding diagnostics (IVD) portfolio

Building Long-Term Scale

* FY29 roadmap targeting ₹390 crore net profit
* Continued investments in manufacturing, distribution, product development, and market expansion
* Diversified business model creating multiple growth avenues across domestic and international markets
* Focused execution to support the Company's long-term growth strategy

Cupid Limited's long-term strategy is supported by two complementary businesses a well-established global institutional export franchise and a rapidly expanding branded consumer business. Supported by manufacturing expansion, a wider product portfolio, growing diagnostics capabilities, pan-India distribution, and continuous product innovation, the Company is strengthening its position across both international and domestic markets while building a scalable and diversified business for the future.

reports.text:
Sakar Healthcare is a company in the pharma industry. It has three revenue segments.

1. CDMO / CMO Services : It is the company's most stable business segment. What they do is they provide end to end services to large pharma companies like product development, scale-up, regulatory support and commercial manufacturing. Some of their customers are Zydus, Emcure, Cipla, etc.

2. Own Brand Exports : This is their primary source of revenue. They manufacture generic medicines under their own brand name, and then export them to over 60+ countries.

3. Out-licensing : They share their formula of how they make a specific medicine to another company, and in return Sakar gets upfront payment, royalty, or shares profits from the company to whom they sell the formula.

Earlier, the company was heavily focused on CDMO services, but now they have started focusing on own brand exports and out-licensing, because they are high margin segment.

All the products which are manufactured in the above three revenue segments can be classified into Non-Oncology and Oncology product segments.

~Non-Oncology products are the traditional products that formed the base of the business. They are manufactured in their Changodar facility. Some examples of these products are Antibiotics, Anti-fungal and cough-syrups.

~Oncology products are the high-margin products which are manufactured in their Bavla facility. These are used to cure cancer disease.

Q4 FY26 Results :

~Revenue grew by 42% YoY to 71 cr.
~EBITDA grew by 67% YoY to 26 cr
~PAT margins expanded by 500 bps to 16%
~The oncology business continues to emerge as a key growth driver, with rising scale, expanding product reach, and increasing export opportunities supporting future momentum.
~Oncology contributed to around 38% of total revenue, while non-oncology contributed to 62%
~Oncology segment contribution almost doubled from last year.

CRDMO | Segment-Wise Industry Map

Key Highlights
- Five distinct segments
- Different growth profiles
- Different entry barriers
- Segment selection matters

Small-Molecule CDMO
- Largest market opportunity.
- Slowest growth segment.
- India's core strength.
- Companies: Divi's, Laurus, Neuland, Cohance.

Biologics CDMO
- Fast-growing global market.
- High technology barriers.
- India's capability gap.
- Companies: Syngene, Anthem, Aurobindo, Laurus Bio.

Peptides & GLP-1
- Fastest-growing segment.
- GLP-1 demand accelerating.
- Purification remains bottleneck.
- Companies: Divi's, Neuland, Laurus, Gland.

Discovery CRO
- Early-stage drug research.
- Sticky customer relationships.
- Funding cycle sensitive.
- Companies: Syngene, Sai Life, Anthem.

Complex & Specialty CDMO
- Highest entry barriers.
- Premium margin business.
- Qualification takes years.
- Companies: Cohance, Piramal, Gland, Concord, Blue Jet.

Segment Comparison
- Growth: Peptides > Biologics > Complex > Discovery > Small Molecules.
- Barriers: ADC/Biologics > Peptides > Complex APIs > Discovery > Commodity APIs.

Key Takeaway
- CRDMO is not a single business model. The best long-term opportunities lie in high-growth, high-barrier segments such as peptides, GLP-1, ADCs and biologics, where companies can sustain stronger pricing power and profitability.

INDIA BANS OVER-THE-COUNTER SALE OF DRUGS CONTAINING OVER 12% ALCOHOL

The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has notified that oral drug formulations containing more than 12% ethyl alcohol (v/v) will no longer be sold over the counter. These medicines will now require a doctor’s prescription, tightening control over products such as certain cough syrups and other alcohol-containing oral medicines to curb misuse and abuse. The notification removes their earlier exemption and brings them under stricter prescription regulations.

INDIA–AUSTRALIA | Long-Term Uranium Supply Agreement

Key Highlights
- Long-term uranium supply secured
- Supports 100 GW nuclear target
- Strengthens energy security
- Expands strategic partnership

Agreement Details
- India signed a long-term agreement with Australia to secure uranium supplies for its nuclear power plants.
- The pact enables Australian uranium exports to India exclusively for peaceful purposes under IAEA safeguards.
- The agreement comes nearly 12 years after the India–Australia Civil Nuclear Cooperation Agreement.
- India currently has 24 nuclear reactors with 8.78 GW of installed capacity.
- The government aims to increase nuclear capacity to 22 GW by FY32 and 100 GW by 2047.
- Both countries also signed agreements covering critical minerals, defence, maritime security, cyber technologies, supply chains and education.
- India and Australia will establish a Critical Minerals Corridor and a Defence Innovation Corridor to deepen strategic cooperation.
- The two nations reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening energy security, resilient supply chains and expanding bilateral trade and investment.

Key Takeaway
- The long-term uranium supply agreement marks a major milestone in India's clean energy strategy, providing fuel security for its ambitious nuclear expansion plans while also deepening India–Australia cooperation across critical minerals, energy, defence, technology and resilient supply chains, reinforcing the broader strategic partnership between the two countries.

NELCO | BSNL Launches Satellite Phone

Key Highlights
- BSNL launched satellite phone
- Priced at ₹1.34 lakh
- Targets remote regions
- Satellite-based connectivity

Business Update
- BSNL has launched a satellite phone in India priced at ₹1.34 lakh.
- The device is designed for use in remote and inaccessible areas without mobile network coverage.
- It offers satellite-based voice calling and emergency communication capabilities, ensuring connectivity where conventional telecom networks are unavailable.

Key Takeaway
- BSNL's satellite phone launch marks a step toward expanding connectivity in remote regions, potentially supporting the adoption of satellite communication services, a segment where Nelco has a presence through its satellite communication business.

Key Takeaway

**AUROBINDO PHARMA | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹1,270
- Valuation seen expensive
- Growth expected to slow

**Brokerage View**
- Citi maintained a **Sell** rating while raising the target price to **₹1,270** from **₹1,020**.
- The recent rally has pushed the stock to around **2 standard deviations above** its 5-year and 10-year average valuation.
- Current valuation already reflects near-term earnings benefits from **foreign exchange gains** and **PLI incentives**.
- Citi expects earnings growth to slow sharply during **FY28–FY29** due to limited pipeline visibility and a high volume base.
- Benefits from the **Lannett** acquisition may be weaker than expected, with its sales declining by around **30%** over the past year, according to IQVIA.
- Growth drivers such as **biosimilars** and the **Merck contract manufacturing business** may not fully offset pressure in the core generics business.
- The company's **PAT CAGR of 2% over five years and 6% over ten years** highlights the limitations of its volume-led growth model.
- At around **20x FY28E EPS**, the stock trades roughly **30% above** its historical average valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi **maintains a Sell rating and raises the target price to ₹1,270**, believing **Aurobindo Pharma's current valuation is stretched**, while **medium-term earnings growth could moderate** due to limited pipeline visibility and slowing momentum in its core generics business.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**DIXON TECHNOLOGIES | JPMorgan View**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight maintained
- Target price at ₹16,700
- Vivo JV approval received
- Revenue estimates upgraded

**Brokerage View**
- JPMorgan maintained an **Overweight** rating with a **₹16,700** target price.
- Dixon has received the long-awaited **Press Note 3 (PN3)** approval for its proposed JV with Vivo.
- The JV will be owned **51% by Dixon** and **49% by Vivo**.
- Vivo sells around **35 million smartphones annually** in India, with about **67% (22 million units)** expected to be manufactured through the JV.
- Management expects the JV to generate an incremental **₹30,000 crore** in revenue, supported by a higher average selling price than Dixon's existing mobile portfolio.
- Commercial operations are expected to begin in **Q3FY27**, around **60–90 days** after approval.
- JPMorgan estimates the JV will contribute **11 million units** in FY27 and **22 million units** each in FY28 and FY29.
- Brokerage increased **FY27–FY29 revenue estimates by 24–39%** and **EPS estimates by 13–18%**, with earnings growth moderated by the JV's **51:49 ownership structure**.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **maintains an Overweight rating with a ₹16,700 target price**, viewing the **Vivo JV approval as a major growth catalyst** that could significantly expand **Dixon's smartphone manufacturing volumes and revenue trajectory** over the next few years.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**DIXON TECHNOLOGIES | UBS View**

**Key Highlights**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price at ₹13,700
- Vivo JV approval received
- Growth outlook strengthened

**Brokerage View**
- UBS maintained a **Buy** rating with a **₹13,700** target price.
- The long-awaited **PN3 approval** for the Dixon–Vivo joint venture is a positive development.
- The approval is expected to support Dixon's growth in **FY27 and FY28**.
- Management had indicated the transaction should be completed within **45–60 days** after receiving PN3 approval.
- Vivo sold around **32 million smartphones** in CY2025.
- Around **two-thirds** of Vivo's smartphone volumes are expected to be shifted to the JV.
- The JV has the potential to manufacture around **1.75 million smartphones per month**.

**Key Takeaway**
- UBS **remains positive on Dixon Technologies with a Buy rating and ₹13,700 target price**, believing the **PN3 approval for the Vivo joint venture is a key milestone** that should drive **higher manufacturing volumes and strengthen earnings growth over FY27–FY28**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

Key Highlights

- Targets range ₹1,800–₹2,500

While Axis Capital, Kotak Securities, JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs remain constructive on the company's long-term prospects, Morgan Stanley remains neutral, whereas Citi and Jefferies continue to hold cautious views, citing growth and valuation concerns.

**LENSKART | JPMorgan Initiates Coverage**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight initiated
- Target price at ₹635
- Strong long-term growth story
- Profitable scalable business

**Brokerage View**
- JPMorgan initiated coverage with an **Overweight** rating and **₹635** target price.
- Lenskart has built a scalable and profitable business addressing India's growing eyewear and eye care market.
- Technology, data analytics and customer-centric execution create strong competitive advantages.
- Store network is expected to expand to **5,600+ stores by FY30** from around **3,300+**, with network densification supporting demand growth.
- Premium products, including progressive lenses, premium frames and smart glasses, are expected to drive higher realizations.
- Growing contribution from high-retention **Gold members** and operating leverage should support earnings growth.
- JPMorgan estimates **21% revenue CAGR** and **41% EPS CAGR** during **FY26–FY30**, along with improving free cash flow and return ratios.
- Key risk remains execution challenges as the business scales further.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **initiates coverage on Lenskart with an Overweight rating and ₹635 target price**, highlighting its **technology-led business model, expanding store network, premiumization strategy and strong earnings growth potential**, making it a compelling long-term compounding story despite premium valuations.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TCS | Morgan Stanley View After Q1 Results**

**Key Highlights**
- Equal-Weight maintained
- Target price at ₹2,200
- Q1 broadly in line
- Margin risks remain

**Brokerage View**
- Q1FY27 results were broadly in line with expectations.
- Better-than-feared Q2 outlook may ease near-term investor concerns.
- EBIT margins could face downside risk versus consensus estimates.
- Revenue growth recovery remains gradual with no clear acceleration yet.
- Limited scope for valuation re-rating until sustained revenue growth improves.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley **maintains an Equal-Weight rating with a ₹2,200 target price**, stating that while **the Q2 outlook is better than feared**, **margin pressures and the lack of a meaningful acceleration in revenue growth** could limit further upside in the stock.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TCS | JPMorgan View After Q1 Results**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target price at ₹2,400
- Q1 broadly in line
- Growth recovery expected

**Brokerage View**
- Q1 revenue was in line to slightly ahead of expectations, aided by strong India business.
- International revenue remained weak as geopolitical uncertainty delayed client decision-making.
- Consumer and telecom verticals saw the biggest impact from slower spending.
- EBIT margin declined **130 bps**, mainly due to annual wage hikes, partly offset by forex gains and operational efficiencies.
- Management expects recovery from Q2, supported by AI-led deal wins and a large technology modernization backlog.
- JPMorgan expects **1% QoQ constant currency revenue growth** from Q2, with stronger momentum in H2FY27.
- Brokerage believes TCS can deliver **2.5–3% YoY constant currency growth** in FY27, potentially matching Infosys and HCLTech.
- At **12.4x valuation**, JPMorgan sees attractive value as growth is expected to accelerate.

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan **maintains an Overweight rating with a ₹2,400 target price**, believing **TCS is well-positioned for a gradual recovery from Q2**, supported by **AI-led deal wins, technology transformation demand, and an improving growth outlook**, while current valuations remain attractive.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

DIXON TECHNOLOGIES | JV With Vivo Mobile India

Key Highlights
- Dixon to hold 51%
- Vivo India to own 49%
- Government approval received
- Multi-brand manufacturing planned

JV Details
- Dixon will hold a 51% stake, while Vivo Mobile India will own 49%.
- Government of India has approved Vivo's investment, enabling incorporation of the JV.
- The joint venture will manufacture Vivo smartphones in India.
- It can also undertake electronics manufacturing for other brands.
- Initial paid-up capital will be ₹5 crore.
- Transaction is expected to be completed within one year, subject to customary approvals.

Key Takeaway
- The Dixon–Vivo joint venture marks a significant expansion in India's electronics manufacturing ecosystem, with government approval, majority ownership for Dixon, and the potential to manufacture products for multiple brands, strengthening Dixon's long-term growth prospects.

Mediators scramble to bring US and Iran back to negotiating table

The US and Iran continue to engage in technical negotiations on nuclear issues with an eye to a diplomatic solution, a US official says, even as President Donald Trump launched a fresh round of retaliatory strikes against Tehran this week.

“The United States is still committed to finding a resolution, and technical talks continue. Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon,” the official said.

The memorandum of understanding, the official added, is “performance-based,” and Iran’s actions, including the Strait of Hormuz not being fully reopen and strikes on ships during the NATO Summit, “constitute failed performance at an unacceptable level.”

“Iran’s attacks on these innocent vessels are acts of terrorism,” the official added.

**NOMURA: Cuts Q1FY27 Estimates for OMCs**

• Nomura expects **HPCL, BPCL and IOCL** to report **Q1FY27 EBITDA losses** of around **₹13,900 crore**, **₹15,800 crore** and **₹17,300 crore**, respectively.

• The losses are driven by **higher crude oil prices**, as OMCs were unable to fully pass on increased fuel costs to consumers.

• **Retail marketing margins** are estimated to decline to a **loss of ₹20–21/litre** in Q1FY27, compared with a **loss of around ₹5/litre** in Q4FY26.

• **Higher Gross Refining Margins (GRMs)** are expected to partially offset the marketing losses.

• **Higher refinery transfer prices** and **rupee depreciation** also impacted profitability.

**DIAMOND POWER INFRA | Revival, Scale & Operating Leverage**

**Capacity Expansion**
• Plant utilization at **25–30%** currently.
• Target utilization **75%** by FY29.
• Minimal capex over next two years.

**Growth Outlook**
• Revenue projected to reach **₹9,240 crore**.
• Revenue CAGR estimated at **70%**.
• PAT projected at **₹840 crore**.
• PAT CAGR estimated at **74%**.

**Growth Drivers**
• Renewable energy demand.
• Transmission and distribution capex.
• Data centre expansion.
• Underground cabling projects.
• Smart city infrastructure.

**Order Book**
• Order book at **₹3,500 crore**.
• Monthly inflows around **₹250–275 crore**.
• Secured major Adani-linked orders.
• Won **₹436 crore** data centre order.

**Balance Sheet**
• NCLT overhang resolved.
• Legal proceedings discharged.
• No significant external bank debt.
• Working capital promoter funded.

**Key Takeaway**
The investment thesis is centered on rising capacity utilization, with strong order inflows and limited capex expected to drive significant operating leverage over the next few years.

**INCRED | DIAMOND POWER INFRASTRUCTURE**

Initiated coverage with a **High-Conviction ADD** rating and a **target price of ₹300**.

The brokerage expects **plant utilisation** to increase from **~25% currently to ~60% by FY28**, driving operating leverage.

InCred forecasts **~5x revenue growth** over the next **three years**, with **EBITDA expected to grow at ~73% CAGR**.

Growth is expected to be supported by strong demand for **MV/EHV cables, underground cabling, data centres**, and **power infrastructure**.

The brokerage also noted that the **NCLT overhang has been resolved**, with the balance sheet expected to strengthen and **minimal capex** required over the next two years.

**Key Takeaway**
InCred sees Diamond Power as a high-growth play, driven by improving utilisation, strong industry tailwinds, and a healthier balance sheet.
